title: Crisis & Consumption
author: Andhika Diskartes
summary: Indonesian consumption is 53% of GDP but middle-class spending power is compressing as manufacturing share of GDP shrinks. Basic human needs persist across centuries; specific companies do not. Down trading reshuffles consumer winners every crisis.
macro: Middle class is the squeeze point — bansos / MBG do not reach them
learnings: Consumer needs ≠ company permanence. Categories survive (food, drink, addictives); companies fail (Nyonya Meneer, Sariwangi pailit despite category permanence).
Down trading is structural in every crisis: premium discretionary collapses (mobil baru 387k → 58k 1997/98, full recovery 2024 — 27 years), staples + illegal/cheaper substitutes win.
INDF/ICBP — Salim 1997/98 triage was correct: kept INDF (profit engine), let go BBCA. INDF ROE 13–15% steady, ICBP ROE ~15% (was 20% pre-cost-spike). Successor generation untested.

ULTJ — "Indonesians are lactose intolerant" hypothesis is wrong; constraint is economic. Q1 2026 revenue +20%, NP +35%; net-cash BS (Rp 3.7T cash / <Rp 2B debt). 100% payout is bijak when product still relevant + cash abundant + no proven innovation runway.

GGRM — Andhika acknowledges thesis 3 years ago was wrong. Cukai treadmill + illegal-cig down trading drained legal volume; price 90,000 → 8,500 (~91% drawdown). Capital trapped in bandara/tol — fails the CEO capital-allocation duty test.
Indonesian consumer emiten normally expensive (P/E ~10x, P/BV >1.5x). Cheap entry only at crisis. Imported-input companies must hedge FX or risk kolaps karena hantaman kurs.
openQuestions: If MBG program is halted, second-order effect on domestic consumption?
If illegal cigarettes are forced into legal taxation, does GGRM rebound — and how much lost share returns?
If manufacturing revival + illegal-cig enforcement + MBG all fail, is consumer sector still a 10–20yr hold?

title: Rupiah Merosot, BBM Menjulang, Bagaimana dengan Saham?
author: Andhika Diskartes
summary: IDR at Rp17,200/USD — worst depreciation in Indonesian history. BBM non-subsidi up 20–66%. Mr Market is fearful: pricing in -50% discounts vs the usual -30%. Separate market perception from company performance.
macro: IHSG -3% on the day of writing
Forex reserves <USD 150B; BI intervening but results not visible
learnings: Three IDR-weakness root causes: BI cannot raise rates (PHK risk), fiscal pressure from MBG, external (Iran-US oil → DXY ↑).
BBM hike (April 2026) hits non-subsidi only: Pertamax Turbo +48%, Dexlite +66%, Pertamax Dex +65%, Bio Solar non-subsidi +20%. Subsidized BBM held as social stabilizer.
BBM has been raised repeatedly since 2000. Stock market kept compounding through every hike. Long-term driver = population growth + purchasing power, not fuel price.
IDR impact is not uniform: CPO/coal exporters benefit (USD revenue, IDR costs); pharma/garment with imported inputs hurt; banks with IDR-only loan books unaffected directly.
In this environment, market prices in -50% discounts (vs usual -30%). Opportunity window wider, but ONLY for businesses still performing — separate market perception from company performance.

Value's note: do not confuse "cheap stock" with "bad business." Some stocks fall to PBV 0.1x because fundamentals are broken — Mr Market is right, not mistaken. Do not buy broken businesses just because they are cheap.
